mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of the unemployment rate (UNRATE) reflect labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings. This negative correlation is a persistent feature: at any level, higher unemployment predicts lower S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is above its sample median, or the rank IC over any 3-year rolling window is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) falls below 3%, market participants interpret this as the onset of an easing cycle that compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods when FEDFUNDS < 3.0 are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) than in periods when FEDFUNDS ≥ 3.0 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) determines real interest rates. High real rates (high FEDFUNDS relative to CPI) are contractionary and negative for equity valuations. The negative signal emerges from the joint level of both series, not from either alone.
failure_condition: In a regression of 60-day forward returns on CPIAUCSL, FEDFUNDS, and their product, the interaction coefficient is not negative and statistically significant (p < 0.10) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a recession regime defined by unemployment rate above 6% and a rising 3-month trend, further declines in payroll employment (PAYEMS) reinforce the negative outlook for earnings and consumption. The joint condition of high/rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS amplifies the negative signal for S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 6.0 and 3-month average of UNRATE increasing
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, 60-day forward returns are not negative in at least 60% of instances, or the average return is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures the output of the manufacturing sector. Declines in INDPRO signal economic contraction and reduced corporate earnings, which are consistently negative for equities. The mechanism operates continuously: lower INDPRO predicts lower forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive on average when INDPRO month-over-month change is negative over any 5-year rolling window, or the rank IC is not negative at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals a sudden drop in consumption, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: any regime but stronger in recession
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following a >1% decline in RSAFS are not lower than the unconditional mean by at least one standard deviation over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling housing starts (HOUST) and declining real GDP (GDPC1) indicates simultaneous weakness in residential investment and overall economic activity. This joint deterioration amplifies the negative signal for corporate earnings and equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rank IC of a combined HOUST+GDPC1 signal (e.g., weighted z-score) is not below -0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight monetary regime defined by the federal funds rate at or above 3%, higher levels of FEDFUNDS act as a drag on economic activity and equity valuations. When the policy rate is elevated, the level of rates is negatively correlated with future returns, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) when FEDFUNDS >= 3.0 over a 10-year sample.
